name: Mike Fay Golf Academy
about: The Mike Fay Golf Academy is a premier golf training facility in Northville, Michigan, led by 2020 Michigan PGA Teacher of the Year Mike Fay. The academy offers a range of instruction programs for adults, juniors, and college players, utilizing TrackMan technology and a simulator powered by TrackMan. Coaching is available through various membership tiers and includes private lessons. While the website does not mention food or beverage services, it emphasizes innovative golf coaching and technology to help players improve their game.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to premium TrackMan technology for precise data analysis
Personalized coaching plans tailored to individual skill levels
Flexible membership options with monthly lesson hours

food_and_drink.notes: No information on food services available.
No information on alcohol services available.

simulator_hardware.notes: Primary launch monitor for simulator and lessons

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you should bring your own clubs for lessons and simulator sessions. The facility does not mention club rentals.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: The website does not mention any food or drink services, so it is unclear if outside items are permitted.

faq.question: How long does an 18-hole simulator session take?
faq.answer: The website does not specify the duration for an 18-hole simulator session.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the Mike Fay Golf Academy offers junior golf instruction programs and has a dedicated section for junior golf development.
